steps.title: Find the relationship between the main claim and what follows
steps.content: The sentence states that cells with identical DNA develop different functions, then immediately explains why—the genome and epigenome have different roles. The sentence needs punctuation that signals “here comes the explanation.”

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for elaboration
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or explanation that follows an independent clause.

After “functions:” the “whereas” clause explains why cells develop different functions despite identical DNA—the genome contains a complete DNA sequence while the epigenome determines which traits are expressed.

Choice
  • A leaves the connection abrupt
  • B places a comma before the subordinate clause (incorrect for “whereas”)
  • C creates an ungrammatical “and
,” structure.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
